title: Harness design for long-running application development
summary: Harness design is key to performance at the frontier of agentic coding. Here's how we pushed Claude further in frontend design and long-running autonomous software engineering.
guidance: We've previously shown that harness design has a substantial impact on the effectiveness of long running agentic coding.
Two insights shaped the harness I built for frontend design.

Design quality: Does the design feel like a coherent whole rather than a collection of parts? Strong work here means the colors, typography, layout, imagery, and other details combine to create a distinct mood and identity.
For more complex tasks, the agent still tends to go off the rails over time.
I started by experimenting on frontend design, where the self-evaluation issue was most visible.

actions.rationale: Sync learnings into a retrieval-first, non-constitutional document.
Potential retrieval and compaction guidance; requires governed review before changing runtime behavior.
Potential frontend quality guidance; keep proposal-only until independently validated.
Potential eval coverage change; requires explicit validation and proposal review.
Potential agent workflow guidance; keep proposal-only to avoid silent runtime drift.
Potential specialist coverage update; keep proposal-only until the need is validated.
